REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION, 501, DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO BE FRIVOLOUS. THE COURT FINDS THAT (1) SEEKING AN APPROVAL OF REJECTION OF THE EXECUTORY AGREEMENT; AND (2) SETTLEMENT NEGOTIATIONS IN THE COURSE OF THE COURT PROCEEDINGS ARE PROTECTED ACTIVITIES. HOWEVER, (1) BREACH OF CONTRACT BY REFUSAL TO PROCEED WITH AN OPTION CONTRACT; AND (2) BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT REACHED IN THE COURSE OF THE COURT PROCEEDINGS ARE NOT PROTECTED ACTIVITIES. PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION 1, 3 AND 4 ARISE OUT OF THE LATTER.ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT ANNE EVANS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT'S 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IN THE INDUCEMENT DISCOVERED IN "JUNE 2012" RELATES BACK TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON JUNE 8, 2015. THEREFORE THE RUNNING OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS NO APPARENT ON THE FACE OF THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT AND ISSUES OF FACT THAT CANNOT BE PROPERLY ADDRESSED ON DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BONNIE MOORE, STEVEN MOORE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is denied. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). ("' the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts.") Additionally, triable issues of fact exist as to fact 6. "If there is even one triable issue of fact, summary judgment cannot be rendered. Wells Fargo Bank v. Kincaid, 260 Cal. App. 2d 120, 123. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Off Calendar May-15-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Freedman, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman (415-956-8100) for Plaintiff/ Opposing party Anne Evans.
Jay Jambeck, Esq. of Leigh Law Group (415-399-9155) for Defendant/Moving party Bonnie Moore.
Court reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, (925-360-6065), Reported.

The Court reads the tentative ruling and informs moving party, the burden is on him. Defendant and Plaintiff cite several paragraphs on their papers. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant'S Special Motion To Strike is taken under submission (as fully reported in the Court Reporter's notes). Plaintiff submits a proposed order in conformity with the tentative.
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, (925-360-6065), Reported. (D501)

*After the hearing, Defendant'S Special Motion To Strike is DENIED. Plaintiffs' causes of action do not arise out of protected activity. The Court does not find Defendants' motion to be frivolous. The Court finds that (1) seeking an approval of rejection of the executory agreement; and (2) settlement negotiations in the course of the court proceedings are protected activities. However, (1) breach of contract by refusal to proceed with an option contract; and (2) breach of the settlement agreement reached in the course of the court proceedings are not protected activities. Plaintiff's causes of action 1, 3 and 4 arise out of the latter.Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
